title: activity — cross-module relations
description: Two aggregates. `Activity` is the vendor-adapted `activity_log` row: polymorphic subject (the thing something happened to) + polymorphic causer (the actor who did it) — plus hard FKs on `tenant_id` and (nullable) `application_id`. `ActivityRetentionPolicy` is a tenant-scoped per-log_name override on top of the config default. Cross-tenant FKs are forbidden; every read is scoped by `BelongsToTenant`.

relations.name: tenant
relations.description: Owning tenant. Auto-filled by BelongsToTenant on save; every read auto-scopes to the caller's tenant.

relations.name: application
relations.description: Application under which the mutation happened. Auto-filled from the `X-Application-Id` request header on tenant-audience writes. NULL for platform-audience rows (a PlatformUser mutation with no active application context).

morphs.name: subject
morphs.description: The thing the activity happened TO. Concrete class resolved via the vendor's morph map (registered by domain modules composing `LogsActivity`). Nullable — a `system.startup` activity may have no subject.

expectedSubjects.note: 'Alice booked Court 3 for tomorrow' — the archetypal activity.
Progress updates, attendance marks, evaluation notes.
Auth events (last_login_at updates), profile updates. NOT for RBAC changes — those route to audit exclusively.

morphs.name: causer
morphs.description: The actor who did the thing. Vendor's `CauserResolver` picks up `auth_user` automatically; explicit `->causedBy($user)` overrides. Nullable — a system-triggered mutation has no human causer (`ReconcileActivityBatchesJob` closing an orphan batch, a scheduled maintenance task).

expectedCausers.note: The primary case — every logged-in tenant user.
Academorix support ops causing mutations on behalf of a tenant. Rare; every one is also captured on the audit stream.
Machine credentials for automated integrations. Actor identity resolvable via `causer_id` → service_account row.

relations.name: tenant
relations.description: Owning tenant. Retention policies are strictly per-tenant.

outbound.description: Cross-references from Activity + ActivityRetentionPolicy without hard FKs.

reads.validated_by: valid_log_name rule (write-time check; unknown names refused with UNKNOWN_LOG_NAME)
reads.description: The `log_name` string is one of the registered feed names — 'default', 'bookings', 'auth', 'branch-management', etc. Catalog is a blueprint fixture that seeds the module's own registry table at boot.
The vendor's `batch_uuid` column groups rows written from one HTTP request. Not FK-constrained — the value is populated by `ActivityBatcher` at request start and cleared at request end.

inbound.description: Downstream consumers compose `LogsActivity` on their models. This module doesn't track them centrally — the vendor's morph map is the registry.
consumers_at_wave_6: billing::Invoice → 'billing' log_name (state transitions only — mutations to money-sensitive fields route to audit exclusively)

notes: Polymorphic subject + causer are the extension seams. The activity module does NOT know that `booking` means Sports' Booking — it just knows a string key registered in the vendor's morph map.
The `tenant_id` FK is HARD — every activity strictly lives inside its tenant. The `application_id` FK is nullable — platform-audience mutations (a PlatformUser action) leave it NULL.

The `Activity` model does NOT compose HasApplication or BelongsToApplication trait. It carries `application_id` per the eight-row exception in `tenancy-columns.md §2` (activity_log is one of the eight rows that carry application_id directly), but the fill happens in the observer, not a trait.
`ActivityRetentionPolicy` is a small aggregate — just tenant scoping. No polymorphism, no causer. Owner + admin CRUD only.
